arabic_source.entry:
٧

مَكِّيَّة قَالَ قَتَادَة إِلَّا قَوْله تَعَالَى (﴿واسألهم عَن الْقرْيَة﴾) الْآيَة فَإِنَّهَا نزلت بِالْمَدِينَةِ
وَلَا نَظِير لَهَا فِي عَددهَا
وكلمها ثَلَاثَة آلَاف وَثَلَاث مئة وَخمْس وَعِشْرُونَ كلمة
وحروفها أَرْبَعَة عشر ألفا وَثَلَاث مئة وَعشرَة أحرف
وَهِي مئتان وَخمْس آيَات فِي الْبَصْرِيّ والشامي وست فِي الْمَدَنِيين والمكي والكوفي
اختلافها خمس آيَات (﴿المص﴾) عدهَا الْكُوفِي وَلم يعدها الْبَاقُونَ (﴿مُخلصين لَهُ الدّين﴾) عدهَا الْبَصْرِيّ والشامي وَلم يعدها الْبَاقُونَ (﴿كَمَا بَدَأَكُمْ تعودُونَ﴾) عدهَا الْكُوفِي وَلم يعدها الْبَاقُونَ (﴿ضعفا من النَّار﴾) عدهَا المدنيان والمكي وَلم يعدها الْبَاقُونَ (﴿الْحسنى على بني إِسْرَائِيل﴾) الثَّالِث عدهَا المدنيان والمكي أَيْضا وَلم يعدها الْبَاقُونَ وَكلهمْ عد (﴿بني إِسْرَائِيل﴾) الأول وَالثَّانِي وَلم يعد (﴿بني إِسْرَائِيل﴾) الرَّابِع و(﴿من الْجِنّ وَالْإِنْس فِي النَّار﴾)
وفيهَا مِمَّا يشبه الفواصل وَلَيْسَ معدودا بِإِجْمَاع أَرْبَعَة مَوَاضِع (﴿فدلاهما بغرور﴾ آل فِرْعَوْن بِالسِّنِينَ) (﴿وخر مُوسَى صعقا﴾ عذَابا شَدِيدا)

english_translation.entry_translation:
Sūrat al-Aʿrāf.

Makkan. Qatāda said: except for His saying, exalted is He, “wasʾalhum ʿani l-qaryah” — the verse — for it was revealed in Medina.

It has no counterpart (naẓīrah) in its verse-count.

Its words are three thousand three hundred and twenty-five, and its letters are fourteen thousand three hundred and ten. It is two hundred and five verses in the Basri and Shami counts, and two hundred and six in the two Madani counts, the Makki count, and the Kufi count.

Its points of disagreement are five verse-endings: “alif-lām-mīm-ṣād” is counted by the Kufi and not counted by the others; “mukhliṣīna lahu l-dīn” is counted by the Basri and the Shami and not counted by the others; “kamā badaʾakum taʿūdūn” is counted by the Kufi and not counted by the others; “ḍiʿfan mina l-nār” is counted by the two Madani counts and the Makki and not counted by the others; and the third occurrence of “al-ḥusnā ʿalā banī isrāʾīl” is likewise counted by the two Madani counts and the Makki and not counted by the others. All of them count the first and second occurrences of “banī isrāʾīl,” and they do not count the fourth occurrence of “banī isrāʾīl,” nor “mina l-jinni wa-l-insi fī l-nār.”

In it there are, by consensus, four places that resemble verse-endings without being counted: “fadallāhumā bi-ghurūr”; “Āla firʿawna bi-l-sinīn”; “wa-kharra mūsā ṣaʿiqan”; and “ʿadhāban shadīdan.”
english_translation.ruus_translation_note: The uploaded witness carries the رؤوس الآي for سورة الأعراف inline, directly after the pseudo-fawasil sentence, rather than under a separate heading. The recovered later-Madani ordinal sequence is complete, but the witness’s displayed numerals are unstable: it begins with an unnumbered first head, skips a displayed 29, inserts an unnumbered “لا تعلمون” after displayed 37, carries displayed 136 blank into the next-page head “بني إسرائيل,” inserts an unnumbered “يعرشون,” and ends with a blank display-total line “٢٠٦”. For that reason, the structured ordinals below are the authoritative sequence, while display_number_in_witness preserves the witness behavior where applicable.

structured_data.source_alignment_note_en: The uploaded witness carries سورة الأعراف as a single section whose entry prose flows directly into an inline ruʾūs sequence rather than a separately headed “ورؤوس الآي” block. The local text witness is sufficient to recover a complete ordered later-Madani head layer, but its displayed numerals are unstable: the first head is unnumbered, displayed 29 is skipped, an unnumbered “لا تعلمون” appears after displayed 37, displayed 136 is blank and carries its head “بني إسرائيل” across the page break, an unnumbered “يعرشون” follows before displayed 137, and the block ends with a blank total line “٢٠٦”. A checked parallel printed witness confirms these features. Although the uploaded page segmentation begins the inline ruʾūs on printed 156, the checked printed witness places the opening head at the foot of printed 155.

classification_claims.claim_ar: مَكِّيَّة قَالَ قَتَادَة إِلَّا قَوْله تَعَالَى (﴿واسألهم عَن الْقرْيَة﴾) الْآيَة فَإِنَّهَا نزلت بِالْمَدِينَةِ

word_count.raw_ar: ثَلَاثَة آلَاف وَثَلَاث مئة وَخمْس وَعِشْرُونَ كلمة

letter_count.raw_ar: أَرْبَعَة عشر ألفا وَثَلَاث مئة وَعشرَة أحرف

disputed_verse_endings.cross_surah_note_en: The source explicitly says that none of the counting authorities count this occurrence as a verse-ending.
The source explicitly says that none of the counting authorities count this phrase as a verse-ending.

structured_data.ruus_al_ayat_numbering_note_en: The ordinal_madani_2_baseline field gives the recovered later-Madani sequence. Because the witness is inline and its display numerals skip, insert, and end with a blank total line, those numerals are preserved only as witness metadata in display_number_in_witness.

ruus_al_ayat.extraction_note_en: Unnumbered first head in the uploaded inline witness; treated as ordinal 1 in the recovered later-Madani sequence.
The witness skips a displayed 29; this head falls one ordinal earlier in the recovered later-Madani sequence.
Unnumbered inserted head after displayed 37 “من النار”; preserved as ordinal 37 in the recovered later-Madani sequence.
Repaired from a page-break split: the numeral fragment closes printed page 156, and the head “بني إسرائيل” is carried at the top of printed page 157.
Unnumbered inserted head immediately after the carried 136 head; preserved as ordinal 137 in the recovered later-Madani sequence.
After the inserted unnumbered head “يعرشون,” the displayed numerals run one step behind the recovered later-Madani ordinals.

extraction_anomalies.description_en: The four pseudo-fawasil in سورة الأعراف are serialized into two paired clusters rather than four neatly itemized lines.
extraction_anomalies.impact_en: The four-item extraction is reliable, but it requires editorial splitting of the serialized phrasing.

extraction_anomalies.description_en: The رؤوس الآي layer for سورة الأعراف is embedded directly after the entry prose and is not introduced by a separate heading node in the uploaded witness.
extraction_anomalies.impact_en: The batch must split the inline ruʾūs logically from the entry before translation and structured extraction.

extraction_anomalies.description_en: The first transmitted head, “للمؤمنين,” appears without a displayed numeral.
extraction_anomalies.impact_en: The recovered ordinal layer treats this as verse-head 1 while preserving the witness’s lack of a displayed numeral.

extraction_anomalies.description_en: The witness jumps from displayed 28 “مهتدون” to displayed 30 “المسرفين,” with no displayed 29 line.
extraction_anomalies.impact_en: The recovered ordinal layer assigns “المسرفين” to ordinal 29 and records the displayed skip as a witness feature.

extraction_anomalies.description_en: After displayed 37 “من النار,” the witness inserts an unnumbered “لا تعلمون” before displayed 38.
extraction_anomalies.impact_en: The inserted head must be preserved explicitly as ordinal 37 in the recovered later-Madani sequence.

extraction_anomalies.description_en: Displayed 136 breaks across the page boundary as a blank numeral fragment on printed 156, with the carried head “بني إسرائيل” at the top of printed 157; an unnumbered “يعرشون” then follows before displayed 137.
extraction_anomalies.impact_en: The carried 136 head and the inserted “يعرشون” must be represented as two separate ordinals in the structured sequence.

extraction_anomalies.description_en: The witness ends with a blank “٢٠٦” total line before the next sura begins.
extraction_anomalies.impact_en: The terminal line is preserved as a display total only and is not treated as an additional head.

extraction_anomalies.description_en: Because the inline witness begins unnumbered, skips displayed 29, inserts unnumbered heads after displayed 37 and after the carried 136 head, and ends with a blank total line, the displayed numerals do not function as a clean independent ordinal layer.
extraction_anomalies.impact_en: The dataset stores recovered later-Madani ordinals together with witness display numbers where present.
